steps.title: Read direction and value
steps.content: The passage explains the relationship between wind strength and the predicted maximum amount of wind-generated electricity. If winds are stronger than forecast, the predicted maximum will be too low, so the actual amount the turbines could generate will be higher than the prediction. The example must therefore use data from the West region showing actual wind generation above the forecasted amount, not below it.

steps.title: Reject wrong graph readings
steps.content: Choice B and Choice C say the winds were so weak that wind generation was less than predicted, which is the opposite direction needed for the example. Choice D has the correct direction because it says the winds were strong enough to generate more electricity, but its amount is about 175 thousand MWh, not the West region's value. The graph supports about 150 thousand more MWh for the West region. The answer is A.
